title: metrosideros polymorpha subsp. glabrifolia
intro: Metrosideros polymorpha subsp. glabrifolia, also known as the New Zealand rātā, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae, native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as a natural antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of Metrosideros polymorpha subsp. glabrifolia are often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
